tools.name: search_experiences
tools.description:
Search for tours and experiences on GetExperience.com. Returns list of matching experiences with id, title, price, location, duration, rating, payment type, photos, booking link.

BOOKING FLOW: search_experiences → get_experience_details → get_experience_schedule → add_to_checkout → create_order

EX

tools.name: get_experience_details
tools.description:
Get full details of a specific experience by ID.

Call when: the user wants to learn more about a tour before booking, or to confirm full description, photos, pricing and availability options.
Call after search_experiences — use id from search results.

All prices are in USD.

RETURNS: full descript

tools.name: get_experience_schedule
tools.description:
Get available time slots for a specific experience on a specific date. Required step before booking.

CRITICAL: Copy configId and startAt EXACTLY from the chosen slot — pass these unchanged to add_to_checkout. Do not modify or recalculate these values. Copy durationMinutes when the slot provides it;

tools.name: get_checkout
tools.description:
Get current cart contents by sessionId.

Use when: you need to confirm what's in the cart before placing the order, or to retrieve checkoutId if it was not saved after add_to_checkout.

sessionId is returned by add_to_checkout — always save it.

RETURNS: checkoutId (required for create_order), list

tools.name: add_to_checkout
tools.description: Add an experience to the cart. Call get_experience_schedule first and take configId, startAt from the chosen slot — pass them here EXACTLY as returned. Also pass durationMinutes when the slot provides it; if it is null, you may omit it and the server will substitute the experience's default duration

tools.name: create_order
tools.description:
Place the final booking order. Last step in the booking flow.

BOOKING FLOW: search → get_details → get_schedule → add_to_checkout → get_checkout → create_order

━━━ TWO PAYMENT MODES ━━━
The right one depends on whether you have an API key:

If you have an X-Api-Key (B2B partner integration):
  Use

tools.name: request_availability
tools.description:
Send an availability request to the host of an ON-DEMAND experience.

Call when: an experience is "available on request" (onDemand: true in search/details, or get_experience_schedule says "available on request") and the user wants it for specific dates. On-demand experiences CANNOT be booked instant

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "get_experience_details" stacks urgency/override words (always, mandatory). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Get full details of a specific experience by ID. Call when: the user wants to learn more about a tour before booking, or to confirm full description, photos, pr…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "get_checkout" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Get current cart contents by sessionId. Use when: you need to confirm what's in the cart before placing the order, or to retrieve checkoutId if it was not saved…

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "create_order" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Place the final booking order. Last step in the booking flow. BOOKING FLOW: search → get_details → get_schedule → add_to_checkout → get_checkout → create_order …

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "create_order" stacks urgency/override words (immediately, always, mandatory). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Place the final booking order. Last step in the booking flow. BOOKING FLOW: search → get_details → get_schedule → add_to_checkout → get_checkout → create_order …

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "request_availability" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Send an availability request to the host of an ON-DEMAND experience. Call when: an experience is "available on request" (onDemand: true in search/details, or ge…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: create_order, request_availability · Private data: search_experiences, get_experience_schedule, create_order · Public sink: create_order, request_availability · Destructive: get_experience_schedule.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: create_order, request_availability · Private data: search_experiences, get_experience_schedule, create_order · Public sink: create_order, request_availability · Destructive: get_experience_schedule

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
